channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wetang'ula Pushes for Voter Registration in Western Kenya Ahead of 2027 Election
item.description: National Assembly Speaker Moses Wetang'ula is leading efforts to boost voter registration in Western Kenya ahead of the 2027 general election. President William Ruto has emphasized the region's critic...

item.title: England's Test Cricket Future: Lessons from Bazball's Failure
item.description: The article critiques the failed Bazball philosophy in English cricket, which was championed by Brendon McCullum and Ben Stokes. Despite initial optimism, the approach lacked consistency and balance, ...

item.title: Prashant Kishor alleges BJP fears his candidature in Bankir assembly by-election
item.description: Prashant Kishor, a prominent political leader, has claimed that the BJP is deeply concerned about his candidacy in the Bankir assembly by-election. The news comes amid heightened political tensions in...

item.title: Discontinued EV Models in the U.S.: Factors Behind the Shifts
item.description: The article details the discontinuation of several electric vehicles (EVs) in the U.S., including Honda's Prologue, Hyundai models, Nissan's Leaf, Polestar's cars, Tesla's S and X, Volkswagen's ID.4 a...

item.title: Tinashe releases new album 'Popstar' and shares single 'Crash Out'
item.description: Tinashe has announced her eighth studio album 'Popstar', set for release on September 25 via Nice Life and Atlantic Records. The 16-track project includes the defiant single 'Crash Out', produced by C...

item.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 9 Palestinians including children amid ongoing conflict
item.description: Israeli military strikes in Gaza City have resulted in the deaths of at least nine Palestinians, including three children, according to hospital officials. A strike on an apartment in the Nasr neighbo...

item.title: Three Bulacan Police Officers Charged Over Mistaken Arrest of Businessman
item.description: Three police officers from Bulacan were dismissed from their posts after attempting to arrest a businessman whom they mistakenly believed was a wanted individual in Baliwag. The incident, which occurr...

item.title: India's Private Rocket Vikram-1 Successfully Reaches Orbit
item.description: India's private space company Skyroot Aerospace achieved a significant milestone with the successful launch of the Vikram-1 rocket on July 18, 2026. The rocket, developed and managed by Skyroot, becam...

item.title: Trust Remains Key in PR Amid AI Advancements
item.description: The article discusses the evolving role of public relations in an era dominated by artificial intelligence. While AI tools are enhancing efficiency in research, data analysis, and communication strate...

item.title: Springboks dominate Wales with 43-0 victory in Durban
item.description: The Springboks delivered a commanding 43-0 victory over Wales at Hollywoodbets Kings Park Stadium in Durban, showcasing their superiority despite early handling errors. The match began with a strong S...

item.title: Tucker Carlson Criticizes Trump's Foreign Policy Impact on GOP Midterm Prospects
item.description: Tucker Carlson, former Fox News host, criticized President Donald Trump's foreign policy decisions, particularly the U.S. war with Iran, during an interview with Mishal Husain on Bloomberg TV. Carlson...

item.title: Uganda School Bus Crash Leaves 24 Dead, Ministry Suspends Excursions
item.description: A school bus owned by King David Junior School in Uganda crashed on Thursday while returning to Kampala after an excursion to Sipi Falls. The incident resulted in the deaths of 22 students and two adu...

item.title: Iran's supreme leader issues warning over escalating US-Iran tensions
item.description: The article details Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Khamenei's stern warning to the United States, cautioning that further military actions against Iran will result in severe consequences. This follow...

item.title: Nasarawa State Raids Illegal Mining Site, Arrests Chinese Nationals and Miners
item.description: Three Chinese nationals and several Nigerian miners were arrested for illegal mining activities in Amba Community, Kokona Local Government Area of Nasarawa State. The State Deputy Governor, Emmanuel A...

item.title: Tragedy strikes family of Kirandeep Kaur after violent attack in London
item.description: Kirandeep Kaur, a 24-year-old mother from India, was fatally stabbed in her London home while sleeping with her husband and infant. Her sister Gursharan, speaking through a translator, described her a...

item.title: Iran's Use of Criminal Networks to Target Canadians is a Growing Security Concern
item.description: The article examines the evolving threat posed by Iran's alleged collaboration with criminal networks to conduct terrorist attacks against Canadians. It references the 1994 Buenos Aires synagogue bomb...

item.title: Tadej Pogacar secures solo victory on Tour de France stage 14, extending his overall lead
item.description: Tadej Pogacar, the four-time champion, delivered a decisive solo victory on the 14th stage of the Tour de France, solidifying his position as the overall leader. The 155.3km stage from Mulhouse to Le ...

item.title: Amarnath and Vaishno Devi Yatra Suspended Due to Adverse Weather Conditions
item.description: The Amarnath and Vaishno Devi yatras have been temporarily suspended due to adverse weather conditions, affecting thousands of pilgrims. Authorities have advised against travel to the pilgrimage sites...

item.title: Four fatalities reported in separate vehicular accidents across Luzon
item.description: Four individuals lost their lives in separate vehicular accidents that occurred in Pangasinan and Pampanga over the past two days. The incidents, which took place in Pozorrubio, Pangasinan; Manaoag; a...

item.title: Republicans Target Disinformation Researchers Amid Political Tensions
item.description: The article discusses how political figures, particularly within the Republican Party, are increasingly focusing on disinformation researchers as part of broader efforts to combat perceived threats to...
